{"step_by_step_thinking": "The drug advisory letter is required to contain information such as drug dosing, frequency, and half-life. Reviewing the options, research papers and articles proving the efficacy of the drug (Option A) are not typically included in the drug advisory letter as it is meant for healthcare professionals and focuses on practical usage information. The date of expiry (Option B), rare but serious life-threatening adverse effects (Option C), and common, not so serious adverse effects (Option D) are all important pieces of information that should be included in the drug advisory letter to ensure safe and effective use of the medication.", "answer_choice": "A"}
